titles.en: Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)
Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ إِذَا حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ بِمِنًى ثُمَّ دَفَنَهُ جَاءَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ كُلُّ شَعْرَةٍ لَهَا لِسَانٌ طَلْقٌ تُلَبِّي بِاسْمِ صَاحِبِهَا .
arabic.text: إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ إِذَا حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ بِمِنًى ثُمَّ دَفَنَهُ جَاءَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ كُلُّ شَعْرَةٍ لَهَا لِسَانٌ طَلْقٌ تُلَبِّي بِاسْمِ صَاحِبِهَا .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Ibrahim Bin Muslim, from Abu Shibl,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Believer, when he shaves off his head in Mina, then buries it, would come on the Day of Judgement, and every hair would have a tongue for it exclaiming Talbiyya in the name of its owner’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Ibrahim Bin Muslim, from Abu Shibl, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Believer, when he shaves off his head in Mina, then buries it, would come on the Day of Judgement, and every hair would have a tongue for it exclaiming Talbiyya in the name of its owner’.

segments: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Ibrahim ibn Muslim from abu Shibl who has said the following: “When a believing person shaves his head and buries his hairs in Mina, on the Day of Judgment every hair comes with a clear tongue, saying Talbiyah (here I am, O Lord, to obey Your command) for the one to whom they belonged.”
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Ibrahim ibn Muslim from abu Shibl who has said the following: “When a believing person shaves his head and buries his hairs in Mina, on the Day of Judgment every hair comes with a clear tongue, saying Talbiyah (here I am, O Lord, to obey Your command) for the one to whom they belonged.”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/185)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/185)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي شِبْلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ رَأْسَهُ بِالْخِطْمِيِّ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِقَهُ قَالَ يُقَصِّرُ وَ يَغْسِلُهُ .
arabic.text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ رَأْسَهُ بِالْخِطْمِيِّ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِقَهُ قَالَ يُقَصِّرُ وَ يَغْسِلُهُ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Aban Bin Taghlub who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Is it for the man that he should wash his head with the hibiscus (flower) before he shaves off his head?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should shorten it and wash it’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Aban Bin Taghlub who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Is it for the man that he should wash his head with the hibiscus (flower) before he shaves off his head?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should shorten it and wash it’.

segments: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr from Mufaddal ibn Salih from Aban ibn Taghlib who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a person who wants to wash his head with marshmallow. He (the Imam) said, ‘He should shorten his hair, then wash.”’
en.sarwar.text: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr from Mufaddal ibn Salih from Aban ibn Taghlib who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a person who wants to wash his head with marshmallow. He (the Imam) said, ‘He should shorten his hair, then wash.”’

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/185)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/185)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ مُفَضَّلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ قَالَ

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ يَحْلِقُ رَأْسَهُ وَ يُقَلِّمُ أَظْفَارَهُ وَ يَأْخُذُ مِنْ شَارِبِهِ وَ مِنْ أَطْرَافِ لِحْيَتِهِ .
arabic.text: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ يَحْلِقُ رَأْسَهُ وَ يُقَلِّمُ أَظْفَارَهُ وَ يَأْخُذُ مِنْ شَارِبِهِ وَ مِنْ أَطْرَافِ لِحْيَتِهِ .

segments: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban Bin usman, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> shaved off his<sup>saww</sup> head on the day of the sacrifice, and he clipped his<sup>saww</sup> nails, and he<sup>saww</sup> took (trimmed) from his<sup>saww</sup> moustache, and from the sides of his<sup>saww</sup> beard’.
en.hubeali.text:
Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban Bin usman, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> shaved off his<sup>saww</sup> head on the day of the sacrifice, and he clipped his<sup>saww</sup> nails, and he<sup>saww</sup> took (trimmed) from his<sup>saww</sup> moustache, and from the sides of his<sup>saww</sup> beard’.

segments: 3. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from ibn Sama‘ah from more than one person from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn abu ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, on the day of al-Nahr (slaughtering the offering, 10th of Dhu al-Hajjah) shaved his head, cut his finger nails, trimmed his mustache and the sides (edges) of his beard.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from ibn Sama‘ah from more than one person from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn abu ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, on the day of al-Nahr (slaughtering the offering, 10th of Dhu al-Hajjah) shaved his head, cut his finger nails, trimmed his mustache and the sides (edges) of his beard.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/185)
gradings.label: مرسل كالموثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/185)

narratorChain.raw: حُمَيْدُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ غَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

parts.text: عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: إِذَا اشْتَرَيْتَ أُضْحِيَّتَكَ وَ وَزَنْتَ ثَمَنَهَا وَ صَارَتْ فِي رَحْلِكَ فَقَدْ بَلَغَ الْهَدْيُ مَحِلَّهُ فَإِنْ أَحْبَبْتَ أَنْ تَحْلِقَ فَاحْلِقْ .
arabic.text: إِذَا اشْتَرَيْتَ أُضْحِيَّتَكَ وَ وَزَنْتَ ثَمَنَهَا وَ صَارَتْ فِي رَحْلِكَ فَقَدْ بَلَغَ الْهَدْيُ مَحِلَّهُ فَإِنْ أَحْبَبْتَ أَنْ تَحْلِقَ فَاحْلِقْ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Myhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you buy your sacrificial animal, and weigh is price, and it comes to be among your rides, so the sacrifice has reached its (appropriate) place. Then if you would like to shave off (your head), so shave’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Myhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you buy your sacrificial animal, and weigh is price, and it comes to be among your rides, so the sacrifice has reached its (appropriate) place. Then if you would like to shave off (your head), so shave’.

segments: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn abu Hamzah who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When you buy your offering animal, measure its price and it becomes as part of your luggage, the offering now has reached its destination, and if you like you can shave your head.’”
en.sarwar.text: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn abu Hamzah who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When you buy your offering animal, measure its price and it becomes as part of your luggage, the offering now has reached its destination, and if you like you can shave your head.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/186)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/186)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ جَهِلَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ مِنْ رَأْسِهِ أَوْ يَحْلِقَ حَتَّى ارْتَحَلَ مِنْ مِنًى قَالَ فَلْيَرْجِعْ إِلَى مِنًى حَتَّى يَحْلِقَ بِهَا شَعْرَهُ أَوْ يُقَصِّرَ وَ عَلَى الصَّرُورَةِ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ جَهِلَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ مِنْ رَأْسِهِ أَوْ يَحْلِقَ حَتَّى ارْتَحَلَ مِنْ مِنًى قَالَ فَلْيَرْجِعْ إِلَى مِنًى حَتَّى يَحْلِقَ بِهَا شَعْرَهُ أَوْ يُقَصِّرَ وَ عَلَى الصَّرُورَةِ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ .

segments: And by his chain, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I asked himasws about a man who was ignorant of (the fact that) he should be shortening from his head, or shave it, until he departed from Mina. Heasws said: ‘So let him return to Mina until he shaves his hair in it, or does ‘Taqseer’ (shortens it, and upon the Sarurah (the first-time performing Al-Hajj) is that he should shave’.
en.hubeali.text: And by his chain, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said, ‘I asked himasws about a man who was ignorant of (the fact that) he should be shortening from his head, or shave it, until he departed from Mina. Heasws said: ‘So let him return to Mina until he shaves his hair in it, or does ‘Taqseer’ (shortens it, and upon the Sarurah (the first-time performing Al-Hajj) is that he should shave’.

segments: 5. Through the same chain of narrators as that of the previous Hadith the following is narrated Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who because of ignorance does not do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) or does not shave his head until he leaves Mina’. He (the Imam) said, ‘He must return to Mina’ to shave his hair or do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) and a Sarurah (first time performing al-Hajj) man must shave his head.’”
en.sarwar.text: 5. Through the same chain of narrators as that of the previous Hadith the following is narrated Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who because of ignorance does not do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) or does not shave his head until he leaves Mina’. He (the Imam) said, ‘He must return to Mina’ to shave his hair or do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) and a Sarurah (first time performing al-Hajj) man must shave his head.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/186)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/186)

narratorChain.raw: وَ بِإِسْنَادِهِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: يَنْبَغِي لِلصَّرُورَةِ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ قَدْ حَجَّ فَإِنْ شَاءَ قَصَّرَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ حَلَقَ
قَالَ وَ إِذَا لَبَّدَ شَعْرَهُ أَوْ عَقَصَهُ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ الْحَلْقَ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ التَّقْصِيرُ .
arabic.text:
يَنْبَغِي لِلصَّرُورَةِ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ قَدْ حَجَّ فَإِنْ شَاءَ قَصَّرَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ حَلَقَ

قَالَ وَ إِذَا لَبَّدَ شَعْرَهُ أَوْ عَقَصَهُ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ الْحَلْقَ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ التَّقْصِيرُ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is befitting for the first-timer that he should shave; and if he had (already) performed Hajj (before), then if he so desires to he can shorten, and if he so desires to he can shave’.
He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And when he adheres his hair or curls (braids) it, so upon him is the shaving, and the shortening is not for him’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is befitting for the first-timer that he should shave; and if he had (already) performed Hajj (before), then if he so desires to he can shorten, and if he so desires to he can shave’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And when he adheres his hair or curls (braids) it, so upon him is the shaving, and the shortening is not for him’.

segments: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is proper for al-Sarura (one who performs al-Hajj for the first time) to shave his head, but if he has already performed al-Hajj he then may shave or do Taqsir (cutting some hair or fingernails and so on).’ He (the Imam) has said that if one has felted or braided his hairs, he must shave.’”
en.sarwar.text: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is proper for al-Sarura (one who performs al-Hajj for the first time) to shave his head, but if he has already performed al-Hajj he then may shave or do Taqsir (cutting some hair or fingernails and so on).’ He (the Imam) has said that if one has felted or braided his hairs, he must shave.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/186)
gradings.label: حسن
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/186)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي  عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: عَلَى الصَّرُورَةِ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ رَأْسَهُ وَ لَا يُقَصِّرَ وَ إِنَّمَا التَّقْصِيرُ لِمَنْ حَجَّ حَجَّةَ الْإِسْلَامِ .
arabic.text: عَلَى الصَّرُورَةِ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ رَأْسَهُ وَ لَا يُقَصِّرَ وَ إِنَّمَا التَّقْصِيرُ لِمَنْ حَجَّ حَجَّةَ الْإِسْلَامِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Upon ‘Al-Saroura’ (the first-timer) is that he shaves off his head and must not do Taqseer (cutting some hair or finger nails). But rather, the Taqseer the is for the one who had (already) performed Hajj, the Hajj of Al-Islam’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Upon ‘Al-Saroura’ (the first-timer) is that he shaves off his head and must not do Taqseer (cutting some hair or finger nails). But rather, the Taqseer the is for the one who had (already) performed Hajj, the Hajj of Al-Islam’.

segments: 7. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Al-Sarura (one who performs al-Hajj for the first time) must shave his head and must not do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on); it is for those who have already performed Hajjahta al-Islam.’”
en.sarwar.text: 7. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Al-Sarura (one who performs al-Hajj for the first time) must shave his head and must not do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on); it is for those who have already performed Hajjahta al-Islam.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/186)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/186)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ مِنْ شَعْرِهِ وَ هُوَ حَاجٌّ حَتَّى ارْتَحَلَ مِنْ مِنًى قَالَ مَا يُعْجِبُنِي أَنْ يُلْقِيَ شَعْرَهُ إِلَّا بِمِنًى
وَ قَالَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ قَالَ هُوَ الْحَلْقُ وَ مَا فِي جِلْدِ الْإِنْسَانِ .
arabic.text:
سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ مِنْ شَعْرِهِ وَ هُوَ حَاجٌّ حَتَّى ارْتَحَلَ مِنْ مِنًى قَالَ مَا يُعْجِبُنِي أَنْ يُلْقِيَ شَعْرَهُ إِلَّا بِمِنًى

وَ قَالَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ قَالَ هُوَ الْحَلْقُ وَ مَا فِي جِلْدِ الْإِنْسَانِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzayl, from Abu Al Sabbah Al Kinany who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about a man who forgot to do Taqseer, and he was a pilgrim of Hajj until he departed from Mina. Heasws said: ‘It does not please measws if he were to throw off his hair except in Mina’.
And heasws said regarding the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [22:29] Then let them fulfill their needful acts. Heasws said: ‘It is the shaving and what is in the skin of the human being’.
en.hubeali.text: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzayl, from Abu Al Sabbah Al Kinany who said, ‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about a man who forgot to do Taqseer, and he was a pilgrim of Hajj until he departed from Mina. Heasws said: ‘It does not please measws if he were to throw off his hair except in Mina’. And heasws said regarding the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [22:29] Then let them fulfill their needful acts. Heasws said: ‘It is the shaving and what is in the skin of the human being’.

segments: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ‘Isma‘il from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl from abu al-Sabbah al-Kinaniy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who forgets to do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) and he is for al-Hajj, until he leaves Mina He (the Imam) said, ’I do not like throwing the hairs in places other than MinaV About the words of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, . . thereafter they must spruce up themselves . . .’[22:29] he (the Imam) said that it refers to shaving and things on a man’s skin.’”
en.sarwar.text: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ‘Isma‘il from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl from abu al-Sabbah al-Kinaniy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who forgets to do Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) and he is for al-Hajj, until he leaves Mina He (the Imam) said, ’I do not like throwing the hairs in places other than MinaV About the words of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, . . thereafter they must spruce up themselves . . .’[22:29] he (the Imam) said that it refers to shaving and things on a man’s skin.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/187)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/187)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي الصَّبَّاحِ الْكِنَانِيِّ قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: رَجُلٍ يَحْلِقُ رَأْسَهُ بِمَكَّةَ قَالَ يَرُدُّ الشَّعْرَ إِلَى مِنًى .
arabic.text: رَجُلٍ يَحْلِقُ رَأْسَهُ بِمَكَّةَ قَالَ يَرُدُّ الشَّعْرَ إِلَى مِنًى .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Al Bakhtary,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who shaves off his head in Makkah. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should return the hair to Mina’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who shaves off his head in Makkah. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should return the hair to Mina’.

segments: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy who has said the following: “About a man who shaves in Makkah abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘He must return (take) his hairs to Mina’.’”
en.sarwar.text: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy who has said the following: “About a man who shaves in Makkah abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘He must return (take) his hairs to Mina’.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/187)
gradings.label: حسن
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/187)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ الْبَخْتَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: السُّنَّةُ فِي الْحَلْقِ أَنْ يَبْلُغَ الْعَظْمَيْنِ .
arabic.text: السُّنَّةُ فِي الْحَلْقِ أَنْ يَبْلُغَ الْعَظْمَيْنِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Giyas Bin Ibrahim,

(It has been narrated) from Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, from his<sup>asws</sup> forefathers<sup>asws</sup>, from Ali<sup>asws</sup> having said ‘The Sunnah regarding the shaving (of the head) is that it should reach the bones’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Giyas Bin Ibrahim,

(It has been narrated) from Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, from his<sup>asws</sup> forefathers<sup>asws</sup>, from Ali<sup>asws</sup> having said ‘The Sunnah regarding the shaving (of the head) is that it should reach the bones’.

segments: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim who has said the following: “Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has narrated from his ancestors, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, who have said, ‘The sunnah about shaving is that it must reach the two bones (down to both temple bones).”’
en.sarwar.text: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim who has said the following: “Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has narrated from his ancestors, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, who have said, ‘The sunnah about shaving is that it must reach the two bones (down to both temple bones).”’

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/188)
gradings.label: موثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/188)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ غِيَاثِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: تُقَصِّرُ الْمَرْأَةُ مِنْ شَعْرِهَا لِعُمْرَتِهَا قَدْرَ أَنْمُلَةٍ .
arabic.text: تُقَصِّرُ الْمَرْأَةُ مِنْ شَعْرِهَا لِعُمْرَتِهَا قَدْرَ أَنْمُلَةٍ .

segments: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of our companions,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Taqseer for a woman from her hair for her Umrah, is to be of the measurement of a finger-nail’.
en.hubeali.text: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of our companions, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Taqseer for a woman from her hair for her Umrah, is to be of the measurement of a finger-nail’.

segments: 11. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) in the case of women is from her hairs of the size of a finger tip.’”
en.sarwar.text: 11. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on) in the case of women is from her hairs of the size of a finger tip.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/188)
gradings.label: صحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/188)

narratorChain.raw: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّا حِينَ نَفَرْنَا مِنْ مِنًى أَقَمْنَا أَيَّاماً ثُمَّ حَلَقْتُ رَأْسِي طَلَبَ التَّلَذُّذِ فَدَخَلَنِي مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( صلوات الله عليه ) إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَأُتِيَ بِثِيَابِهِ حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ
قَالَ وَ قَالَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَ لْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ قَالَ التَّفَثُ تَقْلِيمُ الْأَظْفَارِ وَ طَرْحُ الْوَسَخِ وَ طَرْحُ الْإِحْرَامِ .
arabic.text:
قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّا حِينَ نَفَرْنَا مِنْ مِنًى أَقَمْنَا أَيَّاماً ثُمَّ حَلَقْتُ رَأْسِي طَلَبَ التَّلَذُّذِ فَدَخَلَنِي مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( صلوات الله عليه ) إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَأُتِيَ بِثِيَابِهِ حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ

قَالَ وَ قَالَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَ لْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ قَالَ التَّفَثُ تَقْلِيمُ الْأَظْفَارِ وَ طَرْحُ الْوَسَخِ وَ طَرْحُ الْإِحْرَامِ .

segments: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Nasr who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, ‘When we went from Mina, we stayed for a few days, then I shaved off my head seeking the pleasure, so something entered into me from that (that raised a question into my mind).’  So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, when he<sup>asws</sup> exited from Makkah, they came with his<sup>asws</sup> (normal) clothes, and shaved off his<sup>asws</sup> head’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘And heasws said regarding the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [22:29] Then let them fulfil their needful acts, and let them be loyal to their vows. Heasws said: ‘Tafath (the ‘needful acts’) means the clipping of the nails, and removing the dust, and removing the Ihraam’.
en.hubeali.text: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Nasr who said, ‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Rezaasws, ‘When we went from Mina, we stayed for a few days, then I shaved off my head seeking the pleasure, so something entered into me from that (that raised a question into my mind).’ So heasws said: ‘Abu Al-Hassanasws, when heasws exited from Makkah, they came with hisasws (normal) clothes, and shaved off hisasws head’. He (the narrator) said, ‘And heasws said regarding the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [22:29] Then let them fulfil their needful acts, and let them be loyal to their vows. Heasws said: ‘Tafath (the ‘needful acts’) means the clipping of the nails, and removing the dust, and removing the Ihraam’.

segments: 12. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about our case of leaving Mina’ and staying several days, then shaving my head for pleasure that raised a question in my mind. He (the Imam) said that when abu al-Hassan ‘Alayhi al-Salam, left Makkah, his clothes were brought to him and he shaved his head and said that in the words of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, ‘. . . then they must spruce up and fulfill their vows. . .’ [22:29] ‘Tafath’ means cutting the finger nails, removing dirt and (clothes of) Ihram.’”
en.sarwar.text: 12. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about our case of leaving Mina’ and staying several days, then shaving my head for pleasure that raised a question in my mind. He (the Imam) said that when abu al-Hassan ‘Alayhi al-Salam, left Makkah, his clothes were brought to him and he shaved his head and said that in the words of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, ‘. . . then they must spruce up and fulfill their vows. . .’ [22:29] ‘Tafath’ means cutting the finger nails, removing dirt and (clothes of) Ihram.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/188)
gradings.label: صحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/188)

narratorChain.raw: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ قَالَ

titles.en: Al-Kafi
Chapter 188 – The shaving (of the head) and the shortening (of the hair, nails etc.)

segments: فَأَمَرَ أَنْ يُلَبَّى عَنْهُ وَ يُمَرَّ الْمُوسَى عَلَى رَأْسِهِ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ يُجْزِئُ عَنْهُ .
arabic.text: فَأَمَرَ أَنْ يُلَبَّى عَنْهُ وَ يُمَرَّ الْمُوسَى عَلَى رَأْسِهِ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ يُجْزِئُ عَنْهُ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yaseen Al Zareyr, from Hareyz, from Zurara that,

‘A man from the people of Khurasan proceeded for Hajj, and he was bald-headed and was not good at exclaiming Talbiyya. So Abu Abdullahasws issued a verdict to me for him, and heasws ordered that Talbiyya should be exclaimed on his behalf, and he should pass the blade upon his head, for that would suffice him from it’.
en.hubeali.text: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yaseen Al Zareyr, from Hareyz, from Zurara that, ‘A man from the people of Khurasan proceeded for Hajj, and he was bald-headed and was not good at exclaiming Talbiyya. So Abu Abdullahasws issued a verdict to me for him, and heasws ordered that Talbiyya should be exclaimed on his behalf, and he should pass the blade upon his head, for that would suffice him from it’.

segments: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yasin al-Darir from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “Once a man from Khurasan came for al-Hajj, his head had baldness and could not pronounce Talbiyah correctly. A fatwa was requested about it from abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and he (the Imam) said that Talbiyah must be said on his behalf, and he must pass the blade over his head. It will be sufficient for him.’” NOTE: Fatwa best explains Hadith 3-13 of this and all of the following Chapters.
en.sarwar.text: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yasin al-Darir from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “Once a man from Khurasan came for al-Hajj, his head had baldness and could not pronounce Talbiyah correctly. A fatwa was requested about it from abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and he (the Imam) said that Talbiyah must be said on his behalf, and he must pass the blade over his head. It will be sufficient for him.’” NOTE: Fatwa best explains Hadith 3-13 of this and all of the following Chapters.

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/188)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/188)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ يَاسِينَ الضَّرِيرِ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ أَهْلِ خُرَاسَانَ قَدِمَ حَاجّاً وَ كَانَ أَقْرَعَ الرَّأْسِ لَا يُحْسِنُ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ فَاسْتُفْتِيَ لَهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام )

parts.text: زُرَارَةَ أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ أَهْلِ خُرَاسَانَ قَدِمَ حَاجّاً
كَانَ أَقْرَعَ الرَّأْسِ لَا يُحْسِنُ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ فَاسْتُفْتِيَ لَهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام )
